 {"id":"bd-q59i","title":"User Diagnostics (bd doctor --perf)","description":"Extend cmd/bd/doctor.go to add --perf flag for user performance diagnostics.

Functionality:
- Add --perf flag to existing bd doctor command
- Collect system info (OS, arch, Go version, SQLite version)
- Collect database stats (size, issue counts, dependency counts)
- Time key operations on user's actual database:
  * bd ready
  * bd list --status=open
  * bd show <random-issue>
  * bd create (with rollback)
  * Search with filters
- Generate CPU profile automatically (timestamped filename)
- Output simple report with platform info, timings, profile location

Output example:
  Beads Performance Diagnostics
  Platform: darwin/arm64
  Database: 8,234 issues (4,123 open)
  
  Operation Performance:
    bd ready                  42ms
    bd list --status=open     15ms
  
  Profile saved: beads-perf-2025-11-13.prof
  View: go tool pprof -http=:8080 beads-perf-2025-11-13.prof

Implementation:
- Extend cmd/bd/doctor.go (~100 lines)
- Use runtime/pprof for CPU profiling
- Use time.Now()/time.Since() for timing
- Rollback test operations (don't modify user's database)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:23:11.988562-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-13T22:45:57.26294-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-13T22:45:57.26294-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q59i","depends_on_id":"bd-zj8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:24:06.336236-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-q652","title":"Database pollution in ~/src/dave/vc: 895 issues vs canonical 310","description":"~/src/dave/vc/.beads/beads.db has 895 total issues (675 open, 149 closed), but canonical ~/src/vc/.beads/vc.db has only 310 issues (230 open). This is 585 extra issues - likely pollution from other repositories.

Need to:
1. Identify which issues are polluted (use detect-pollution)
2. Compare issue IDs between dave/vc and canonical vc databases
3. Determine pollution source (beads repo? other repos?)
4. Clean up polluted database
5. Root cause: why did pollution occur?","notes":"Investigation findings so far:
- Polluted DB (~/src/dave/vc/.beads/beads.db): 241 issues (180 open, 43 closed)
- Canonical DB (~/src/vc/.beads/vc.db): 310 issues (230 open, 62 closed)
- Contradiction: Polluted has FEWER issues, not more (241 < 310, diff of 69)
- Only 1 unique ID in polluted: vc-55fi
- All source_repo fields are set to "." in both databases
- Issue description claims 895 issues in polluted vs 310 canonical - numbers don't match current state
- Possible: Pollution was already partially cleaned, or issue description refers to different database?","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-07T00:07:37.999168-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T00:13:32.179396-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-07T00:13:32.179396-08:00"}
